channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: The Misconceptions of Tech Work | The Future of Work in Africa
item.description: On February 12, 2026, I had the privilege of being a guest on the 𝗕𝗿𝗮𝘇𝗲𝗻 𝗘𝘅𝗰𝗵𝗮𝗻𝗴𝗲 𝗣𝗼𝗱𝗰𝗮𝘀𝘁 with 𝗩𝗮𝗻𝗲𝘀𝘀𝗮 𝗘𝗺𝗲𝗮𝗱𝗶. The episode is titled: “𝙏𝙝𝙚 𝙈𝙞𝙨𝙘𝙤𝙣𝙘𝙚𝙥𝙩𝙞𝙤𝙣𝙨 𝙤𝙛 𝙏𝙚𝙘𝙝 𝙒𝙤𝙧𝙠 | 𝙏𝙝𝙚 𝙁𝙪𝙩𝙪𝙧𝙚 𝙤𝙛 𝙒𝙤𝙧𝙠 𝙞𝙣...

item.title: Employees across OpenAI and Google support Anthropic’s lawsuit against the Pentagon
item.description: On Monday, Anthropic filed its lawsuit against the Department of Defense over being designated as a supply chain risk. Hours later, nearly 40 employees from OpenAI and Google - including Jeff...

item.title: Gestor de Tareas Full-Stack (Go + Angular)
item.description: 🚀 Saliendo de la zona de confort: Mi viaje Full-Stack de Go a Angular. Como desarrollador Front-end, disfruto creando interfaces fluidas y reactivas. Sin embargo, siempre tuve la curiosidad de...

item.title: OpenAI and Google Workers File Amicus Brief in Support of Anthropic Against the US Government
item.description: Google DeepMind chief scientist Jeff Dean is among the AI researchers and engineers rushing to Anthropic's defense.

item.title: Thomas Selfridge: The First Airplane Fatality

item.title: Why End-to-End Encryption Cannot Protect Infrastructure Metadata
item.description: The recent incident involving Proton and the FBI is not a technical failure of encryption. It is a harsh reminder of a fundamental architectural truth: end-to-end encryption protects the payload,...

item.title: Reddit-style threaded comment section I built with React / Next.js
item.description: I built a simple Reddit-style threaded comment section using React and Next.js. Features: Nested replies Vertical thread lines Hover highlighting for comment threads Reply forms inside comments...

item.title: Most AI Agent Frameworks Treat Chat as a Runtime. That’s the Problem
item.description: I am building Tandem because the next real step for AI systems is not better chat. It is coordinated autonomous work. Assistants are useful. OpenClaw is a strong personal assistant. Tandem is...

item.title: Bluesky CEO Jay Graber will step aside
item.description: Jay Graber became the first CEO of Bluesky in 2021 when the network and its AT Protocol spun out from life as a Twitter research project to go independent, but now she's leaving that role, as...

item.title: Una idea del siglo XIX que aparece en su compilador, sus tests y el diseño de su sistema
item.description: Introducción En 1872, el matemático Felix Klein se enfrentaba a un problema que mantenía en desacuerdo a los matemáticos de su época: habían proliferado geometrías distintas, la euclidiana, la...

item.title: TypeScript 5.x en 2026: Las Funcionalidades que Realmente Importan en Producción
item.description: Llevo casi dos años usando TypeScript 5.x activamente — primero en un proyecto personal, luego en producción con un equipo de seis personas construyendo una plataforma SaaS B2B. No he probado cada...

item.title: Testing Dev.to Automation with TechResolve
item.description: Welcome to TechResolve! This is a test post generated to verify that our automated publishing system is working correctly. What we are testing: GitHub Actions: Triggering the publish-devto.yml...

item.title: Serverless vs Contenedores en 2026: Guía Práctica de Decisión para Equipos de Backend
item.description: El año pasado mi equipo — éramos cinco ingenieros en ese momento — llevaba casi dos años con toda la infraestructura de backend en AWS Lambda. Funciones Python para procesar eventos, APIs...

item.title: You can get three months of Disney Plus and Hulu for $15
item.description: If you’re looking for a way to stream the Oscars this weekend, or catch up on nominees like The Secret Agent, the Disney Plus and Hulu bundle is worth a look. The ceremony will stream live on Hulu...

item.title: Apple’s new M5 Max feels like a huge upgrade if you bought your laptop three years ago
item.description: We've been busy testing many new MacBooks, ranging from the new $1,099 M5 MacBook Air, going all the way up the $6,149 16-inch MacBook Pro with the M5 Max chip. While these computers are identical...

item.title: Apple Studio Display XDR review: pro at a premium
item.description: It's been almost exactly four years since Apple released the 5K Studio Display that so many wanted, even if it didn't really deliver as a high-end display. Apple has now revamped the Studio...

item.title: One of this rugged phone’s cameras is a pop-out action cam
item.description: What if your smartphone's camera wasn't locked to the back of the device? Honor's Robot Phone, which we got to see in action at MWC 2026, features a camera attached to a stabilized gimbal arm that...

item.title: Anthropic launches code review tool to check flood of AI-generated code
item.description: Anthropic launched Code Review in Claude Code, a multi-agent system that automatically analyzes AI-generated code, flags logic errors, and helps enterprise developers manage the growing volume of...

item.title: ‘Cash Apples’ is giving away $500,000 to people who click on trees in a web browser
item.description: Want to make real money just by clicking on virtual trees? Starting today at 1PM PT / 4PM ET, residents of the United States can begin an online hunt for golden apples in a cutesy web game -...

item.title: Granite 4.0 1B Speech: Compact, Multilingual, and Built for the Edge
Malicious npm Package Posing as OpenClaw Installer Deploys RAT, Steals macOS Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a malicious npm package that masquerades as an OpenClaw installer to deploy a remote access trojan (RAT) and steal sensitive data from compromised hosts....

item.title: OpenAI acquires Promptfoo to secure its AI agents
item.description: This deal underscores how frontier labs are scrambling to prove their technology can be used safely in critical business operations.

item.title: Anthropic sues Defense Department over supply-chain risk designation
item.description: Anthropic filed suit against the Department of Defense on Monday after the agency labeled it a supply chain risk. The complaint calls the DOD's actions "unprecedented and unlawful."

item.title: Google’s latest Pixel Watches have fallen to their lowest prices ever
item.description: With longer days and warmer weather on the way, it’s a good time to take your gym routine outside. Luckily, Google’s Pixel Watch 4 can help you track all your outdoor fitness activities, and right...

item.title: Anthropic is suing the Department of Defense
item.description: Anthropic has sued the US government over its designation as a supply-chain risk, the latest move in a weekslong battle between it and the Pentagon over the acceptable use cases for its military...

item.title: Battlefield 6 teams hit with layoffs despite ‘biggest launch in franchise history’
item.description: Even a record-breaking launch can't seem to save developers from layoffs. According to a report from IGN, the various teams behind Battlefield 6 have been hit with an unspecified number of...

item.title: Qualcomm’s partnership with Neura Robotics is just the beginning
item.description: Neura Robotics is going to build new robots on top of Qualcomm's new IQ10 processors that were released at CES.

item.title: Anthropic Sues Department of Defense Over Supply-Chain-Risk Designation
item.description: The Claude chatbot developer says the Trump administration overstepped by escalating a contract dispute into a federal ban on the company’s technology.

item.title: How AI is turning the Iran conflict into theater

item.title: Sandberg, Clegg join Nscale board as this ‘Stargate Norway’ startup hits $14.6B valuation
item.description: Nvidia-backed British AI infrastructure startup Nscale has raised another megaround of $2 billion.
